prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-zStylz.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: choose event-driven vs direct calls architecture, define core types/interfaces, implement base notification service, create email adapter stub

parameters.summary: Notification system uses event-driven architecture via EventBus exclusively, with pluggable NotificationAdapter interface for delivery channels
parameters.context: The codebase has an existing EventBus and a CallbackRegistry. The NotificationService currently mixes both patterns. The task requires choosing ONE pattern and building a foundation that 11 other developers will extend.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is already the primary communication pattern in the codebase (OrderService uses it for order:created). It supports async handlers, provides an event log for debugging/testing, and enables loose coupling. The adapter pattern on top allows adding new channels (email, webhook, SMS) without modifying the core service. All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit() and be delivered through NotificationAdapter implementations.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Tighter coupling, synchronous-only, no event log, and the codebase comments already flag this as the pattern to remove
Explicitly flagged as inconsistent in existing code comments. Makes the codebase harder to maintain and reason about.

constraints: All notifications must go through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls for notification triggers
All delivery channels must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
NotificationService subscribes to events and delegates to registered adapters
The CallbackRegistry pattern is removed from notification flow
assumptions: 11 more developers will build on this foundation
New notification channels (webhook, SMS, in-app) will be added by other agents
The EventBus remains in-process (no external message broker needed yet)

parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */
parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture: All order lifecycle events (creation, status changes) are
 * emitted via EventBus exclusively. The NotificationService and any other
 * interested listeners subscribe to these events independently.
 */

parameters.summary: All notification triggers must go through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls or CallbackRegistry for notifications
parameters.detail: The notification system uses event-driven architecture exclusively. Domain services (OrderService, UserService, etc.) emit typed events via EventBus. NotificationService subscribes to those events and delegates delivery to registered NotificationAdapter implementations. The CallbackRegistry pattern is no longer used for notifications. To add a new notification trigger: 1) Define the event type in src/events/event-types.ts, 2) Emit it from the relevant service, 3) Add a handler in NotificationService.

parameters.summary: All notification delivery channels must implement the NotificationAdapter interface from src/models/notification.ts
parameters.detail: NotificationAdapter has two requirements: a readonly `channel` property (NotificationChannel type) and an async `send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>` method. Adapters go in src/adapters/ and are registered with NotificationService.registerAdapter(). See EmailNotificationAdapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts as the reference implementation. One adapter per channel — later registrations replace earlier ones.

parameters.summary: CallbackRegistry (src/utils/callback-registry.ts) is no longer used for notifications — do not add new notification callbacks there
parameters.detail: The statusChangeCallbacks instance in callback-registry.ts was previously used for order status change notifications. This has been replaced by EventBus events. The file still exists but is not imported by any service or test. New notification triggers should use EventBus exclusively.

parameters.summary: Agent-1 complete: Notification system foundation implemented with event-driven architecture, adapter pattern, and email adapter stub
parameters.detail:
Implemented:
- src/models/notification.ts: Core types (NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationAdapter interface)
- src/adapters/email.adapter.ts: Email delivery adapter implementing NotificationAdapter (stub with in-memory tracking)
- src/services/notification.service.ts: Refactored to use EventBus exclusively, supports pluggable adapters via registerAdapter()
- src/services/order.service.ts: Migrated status change events from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
- src/index.ts: Exports updated for all new types and adapter
- tests/services/notification.service.test.ts: Updated tests including adapter integration tests
- tests/services/order.service.test.ts: Updated to reflect unified EventBus pattern

All 72 tests pass. TypeScript compiles cleanly.

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement event-driven notification system with adapter pattern

Unify notification architecture on EventBus exclusively, removing the
mixed CallbackRegistry pattern. Add NotificationAdapter interface for
pluggable delivery channels and EmailNotificationAdapter as reference
implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/models/notification.ts b/src/models/notification.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..6c00a58
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/models/notification.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@
+/**
+ * Notification model types for the event-driven notification system.
+ *
+ * Architecture: All notifications flow through the EventBus. The NotificationService
+ * subscribes to domain events, builds NotificationPayload objects, and delegates
+ * delivery to registered NotificationAdapter implementations.
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - All delivery channels must implement the NotificationAdapter interface.
+ * - NotificationType must be extended when new notification categories are added.
+ * - NotificationChannel must be extended when new delivery mechanisms are added.
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Categories of notifications the system can send.
+ * Extend this union type when adding new notification categories.
+ */
+export type NotificationType =
+  | 'order_confirmation'
+  | 'order_status_update'
+  | 'welcome'
+  | 'account_updated';
+
+/**
+ * Delivery channels for notifications.
+ * Each channel has a corresponding NotificationAdapter implementation.
+ */
+export type NotificationChannel = 'email' | 'webhook' | 'in_app' | 'sms';
+
+/**
+ * The payload passed to adapters for delivery.
+ * This is the canonical shape that all adapters receive.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPayload {
+  /** Unique notification ID */
+  id: string;
+  /** What kind of notification this is */
+  type: NotificationType;
+  /** Which channel to deliver through */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Recipient identifier (email address, user ID, webhook URL, etc.) */
+  recipient: string;
+  /** Notification subject/title */
+  subject: string;
+  /** Notification body content */
+  body: string;
+  /** Optional structured metadata for adapter-specific needs */
+  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+  /** When the notification was created */
+  createdAt: Date;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Result of a notification delivery attempt.
+ */
+export interface NotificationResult {
+  /** The notification that was sent */
+  notificationId: string;
+  /** The channel it was delivered through */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Whether delivery succeeded */
+  success: boolean;
+  /** Error message if delivery failed */
+  error?: string;
+  /** When delivery was attempted */
+  deliveredAt: Date;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Interface that all notification delivery adapters must implement.
+ *
+ * To add a new notification channel:
+ * 1. Add the channel name to the NotificationChannel type above.
+ * 2. Create an adapter class implementing this interface in src/adapters/.
+ * 3. Register it with NotificationService via registerAdapter().
+ */
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** Which channel this adapter handles */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this channel.
+   * Must return a NotificationResult indicating success/failure.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}
